title: podman rootless causes Error: cannot re-exec process
body:
I am trying to add all the debugging I have found from all the other tickets opened with this error

```bash
$ podman --version
podman version 2.2.1
```

```bash
$ podman info --log-level=debug
```

```bash
$ ls /proc/self/ns; cat /proc/self/uid_map
cgroup  ipc  mnt  net  pid  pid_for_children  user  uts
         0          0 4294967295
$ cat /proc/sys/user/max_user_namespaces
63907
```

comments.body:
could you configure some additional IDs for your user in `/etc/subuid` and `/etc/subgid`?

Are newuidmap/newgidmap installed on your system?

A couple of things to look into:

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md#9-newuidmap-missing-when-running-rootless-podman-commands

https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md#19-error-creating-libpod-runtime-there-might-not-be-enough-ids-available-in-the-namespace

```
$ cat /etc/subuid
digitalxero:110000:65536
$ cat /etc/subgid
digitalxero:110000:65536
$ podman run -ti fedora sh
Error: cannot re-exec process
$ podman unshare cat /proc/self/uid_map
Error: cannot re-exec process
$ newuidmap -?
usage: newuidmap <pid> <uid> <loweruid> <count> [ <uid> <loweruid> <count> ] ...
$ newgidmap -?
usage: newgidmap <pid> <gid> <lowergid> <count> [ <gid> <lowergid> <count> ] ...
```

When I deleted `/tmp/run-1000/libpod/tmp/pause.pid` I now get
```
$ podman info
Error: kernel does not support overlay fs: 'overlay' is not supported over extfs at "/opt/podman/containers/storage/overlay": backing file system is unsupported for this graph driver
```

Delete my storage.conf and poof it works again. So should I add an entry to https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md


---
### 27) podman aborts with Error: cannot re-exec process

```console
$ podman version
Error: cannot re-exec process
```

#### Solution

  * Remove the `pause.pid` file `$ rm /tmp/run-1000/libpod/tmp/pause.pid`

```console
$ podman version
Version:      2.2.1
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Dec 31 16:00:00 1969
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
Do other commands work after removing the `pause.pid`?

> Do other commands work after removing the `pause.pid`?

Yes, everything works as expected now.
Great, thanks! I am going to close it assuming it was temporary hiccup.

I know this solution works, but do we have any permanent solution for this? After a gap of few days this issue again occurs, and we have to delete this pause.pid file. There should be a permanent fix to this issue.

In my case, the file 'pause.pid' doesn't exists.
Inside /tmp/run-1000/libpod/tmp/ dir, have a 'alive.lck' file and 3 dirs: events, exits, socket.
I tried to remove this 'alive.lck' file, but it didn't worked for me.

My podman version is 3.0.1 running at Debian 11.
can you please provide more information?  How was the systemd session created?

I'm running podman in Debian over wsl (Win10), so it doesn't have a systemd.
I found the problem. The wsl version were 1, and it should be 2. Now works good. Thank you!

@rhatdan I'd like to know just like @vinusorout whether there's any permanent fix for this. We're using Podman for our CI and every few days one of the Agents breaks because of this. This is quite annoying and our devs are not happy about that.

Thanks in advance.
@enyineer Please open a new issue. Do not add to old issues.
